etymology.description: Depicts a sacred composite creature with features of snake, crocodile, fish, deer, etc. -> Large-mouthed beast head, S-shaped body, possibly scale-like patterns -> Head ornament elements resemble antlers or clan emblems -> Evolved into separate head (left) and body (right) parts in writing
etymology.interpretation: The character 龍 (lóng, 'dragon') is a pictogram representing a mythical creature combining attributes like scales, a beast head, and an elongated body. Its depiction included antler-like ornaments or clan symbols, suggesting its status as a sacred animal in ancient China. The evolution of the character involved separating the head and body elements, reflecting practical adaptations in script forms over time.

examples.chinese: 在中国文化中，龙象征着权力和幸运(在中國文化中，龍象徵著權力和幸運)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Zhōngguó wénhuà zhōng, lóng xiàngzhēng zhe quánlì hé xìngyùn.
examples.english: In Chinese culture, the dragon symbolizes power and luck.

examples.chinese: 龙年被认为是特别吉祥的一年(龍年被認為是特別吉祥的一年)。
examples.pinyin: Lóngnián bèi rènwéi shì tèbié jíxiáng de yī nián.
examples.english: The Year of the Dragon is considered especially auspicious.

examples.chinese: 传说中的龙能够呼风唤雨(傳說中的龍能夠呼風喚雨)。
examples.pinyin: Chuánshuō zhōng de lóng nénggòu hū fēng huàn yǔ.
examples.english: Legendary dragons have the ability to call forth wind and rain.
